metadata.title: Build an AGENTS.md Generator for Any Coding Project with Codex
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: Every session where you re-explain your project's conventions is time you spent paying for a file you could have created in 20 minutes.

AGENTS.md is a communication protocol between you and a stateless collaborator. Codex starts every session with no memory of the last. The 20 minutes it takes to write AGENTS.md is repaid at the start of every subsequent session — but only if the file contains what Codex can't infer from the code, not what it already knows.

claude "Read this project directory and generate a first-draft AGENTS.md. Five sections: Stack, Style, Architecture, Environment, Lessons learned. Tag each entry: INFERABLE if Codex knows it from code, or NEEDS HUMAN INPUT if not visible in code. Under 80 lines." < project_files_listing.txt

props.command: claude "Read this project directory and generate a first-draft AGENTS.md. Five sections: Stack, Style, Architecture, Environment, Lessons learned. Tag each entry: [INFERABLE] or [NEEDS HUMAN INPUT]. Under 80 lines." < project_files_listing.txt

beats.narration_text: agents_md_generator.py — reads project directory, generates draft with INFERABLE/NEEDS HUMAN INPUT tags, produces cleaned final removing INFERABLE entries.

props.code:
# reads project directory, tags entries, produces cleaned AGENTS.md
import agents_drafter

draft = agents_drafter.generate(project_dir='.')
tagged = agents_drafter.tag(draft)  # INFERABLE | NEEDS HUMAN INPUT
cleaned = agents_drafter.filter_inferable(tagged)
agents_drafter.write(cleaned, path='AGENTS.md')

beats.narration_text: Draft generated: 23 entries. 14 tagged INFERABLE — GDScript snake_case convention, Godot 4.x API, standard scene structure — all visible in the code. 9 tagged NEEDS HUMAN INPUT: the export key path, the lesson about packed scenes copying by value, the deployment target, and 6 architectural decisions not derivable from code structure. Cleaned final: 9 entries, under 40 lines.
Run the generator on the same project 6 weeks later after lessons have been added.

Lessons learned section now has 4 entries — packed scenes copy by value; export key requires absolute path; AnimationPlayer nodes must be child of animated node; delta accumulation needed for frame-independent motion. Stack, Style, Architecture, Environment sections unchanged — they stabilized after the first run. The file grew in exactly the section designed to grow.

The INFERABLE/NEEDS HUMAN INPUT split is the key discipline. An AGENTS.md full of things Codex already knows from code is noise — it makes the file longer without making Codex more accurate. The productive entries are the ones that aren't in the code: past mistakes, deployment quirks, architectural decisions made and closed.
Next: turn any natural-language task description into a five-element Codex specification.
Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist plus Educational AI. nikbearbrown.com.
